name: São Tomé and Príncipe

outbound_official.note: no official São Tomé and Príncipe government outbound visa-list page located; rely on destination-side data

visa_types.name: Autorização de Entrada (e-Visa / Entry Authorization)
visa_types.purpose: Grants foreign nationals permission to enter São Tomé and Príncipe for tourism, leisure, or short visits. Applied for online via the official SMF e-Visa portal.
visa_types.notes: Processing is 7 business days (7 dias úteis). Payment is collected at the border upon arrival, not during the online application. Requires: scanned passport in PDF (valid 3+ months beyond intended stay), return ticket, proof of accommodation or contact in STP, and proof of financial means (€100/day minimum at border). Minors additionally require birth certificate and guardian declarations. No information changes allowed after submission. Legal basis: Decreto Lei 11/2009 and Lei 5/2008. The official portal does not differentiate tourist from other short-stay categories - a single e-visa entry authorization covers all short-stay purposes.

visa_types.name: Autorização de Entrada (e-Visa / Entry Authorization) - Business
visa_types.purpose: Allows foreign nationals to enter São Tomé and Príncipe for business meetings, commercial activities, or investment-related visits. Applied via the same SMF e-Visa portal as tourist entry.
visa_types.notes: The SMF e-visa portal (smf.st/evisa) uses a single entry authorization form for all short-stay purposes. No separate business visa category is listed officially; business travelers use the same Autorização de Entrada. Payment at border. Same documentation requirements as tourist entry authorization.

visa_types.name: Visto de Trânsito (Transit Visa)
visa_types.purpose: Permits foreign nationals to transit through São Tomé and Príncipe en route to a third country.
visa_types.notes: Transit is explicitly referenced on the SMF e-visa page as a separate use case. Applicants transiting must hold valid entry authorization for their destination country. Applied via the same e-visa portal. Specific fee and max transit duration not published on the official portal.

visa_types.name: Visto de Residência (Residence Visa)
visa_types.purpose: Authorizes foreign nationals to reside in São Tomé and Príncipe on a longer-term basis, covering work, family reunification, retirement, and other non-tourist stays.
visa_types.notes: Residence visa (Visto de Residência) is available through the eCidadão system referenced on the main SMF website. Specific subcategories (work, student, family, retirement) are not enumerated on the official web portal. Detailed requirements, fees, and processing times are not published online; applicants should contact SMF directly at (+239) 2222098 or direccaosmf@gmail.com. This covers all long-stay/residency-based authorizations not served by the short-stay e-visa.

visa_types.name: Isenção de Visto - Passaporte Diplomático/Serviço (Diplomatic/Service Passport Exemption)
visa_types.purpose: Visa-free entry for holders of diplomatic and service passports from CPLP member states and countries with bilateral diplomatic agreements.
visa_types.notes: Presidential Decree n. 21/2002 (20 December 2002) ratifies CPLP agreement suppressing visas for diplomatic and service passports among CPLP member states (Angola, Brazil, Cabo Verde, Equatorial Guinea, Guinea-Bissau, Mozambique, Portugal, Timor-Leste). China diplomatic, service, and public-affairs passport holders also receive 30-day visa-free entry under bilateral agreement (Decree n. 09/2017, in force 2018-02-03). South Africa diplomatic/service passport agreement was approved November 2024. Duration for CPLP diplomatic/service passports not specified in available official sources.

visa_on_arrival.notes: Holders of ordinary (non-diplomatic/non-service) Chinese passports may obtain a visa on arrival for 15 days. This is consistent with Wikipedia's visa policy page and aggregator sources. Note: Chinese diplomatic, service, and public-affairs passport holders get a separate 30-day visa-free exemption under the China-STP bilateral agreement (see conditional_access).

e_visa.notes: An e-Visa (Autorização de Entrada) can be applied for online via the official SMF e-Visa portal (smf.st/evisa). Processing time is up to 7 working days. Payment is made at the border upon arrival. Requires: scanned passport (PDF), reservation details, return ticket, and proof of means of subsistence (100 EUR/day). Applies under Decreto Lei 11/2009 and Lei 5/2008. Nationalities that already qualify for visa-free entry or visa on arrival may still use the e-Visa as an alternative route.

visa_policy.notes: São Tomé and Príncipe implemented a visa exemption policy to promote tourism and business. Exemptions are divided into: (1) 15-day visa-free entry (extendable to 90 days) for CPLP countries, EU, and a specific list of countries/territories - the official SMF notice (idnews=20) is the authoritative source; (2) holders of valid Schengen or US visas (or resident permits per Wikipedia) also qualify for the 15-day exemption; (3) bilateral cooperation agreements allowing longer stays (up to 90 days) for specific African nations; (4) China ordinary passport holders may obtain a visa on arrival for 15 days (not on official SMF page, sourced from Wikipedia). All other nationalities must apply via the e-Visa portal at smf.st/evisa or at an embassy/consulate. Note: Brazil, San Marino, and Equatorial Guinea appear on aggregator lists (saotomeislands.com, Wikipedia) but are NOT explicitly listed on the official SMF notice (idnews=20) - Brazil and Equatorial Guinea are CPLP members and thus covered by the CPLP group entry; San Marino's status is unconfirmed on official sources.

credential.subtype: any valid Schengen visa or resident permit

conditional_access.conditions: Passport must be valid for more than 3 months. Visa or resident permit must be valid. Official SMF text (Portuguese): 'Um passaporte com validade superior a três meses; Um visto Schengen válido ou um visto válido dos Estados Unidos.' Wikipedia also confirms resident permits (not just visas) from the US or Schengen area qualify.

credential.subtype: any valid US visa or resident permit

conditional_access.conditions: Passport must be valid for more than 3 months. US visa or resident permit must be valid. Official SMF text (Portuguese): 'Um passaporte com validade superior a três meses; Um visto Schengen válido ou um visto válido dos Estados Unidos.' Wikipedia also confirms resident permits (not just visas) from the US or Schengen area qualify.

CPLP member state diplomatic and service passport holders. Presidential Decree n. 21/2002 of 20 December 2002 ratifies the Agreement on Suppression of Visas in Diplomatic Passports and Special Service Passports between the Governments of the Member Countries of the Community of Portuguese Speaking Countries (CPLP). Duration not specified in available sources.

Holders of Chinese diplomatic passports, service passports, and passports for public affairs. Agreement entered into force 2018-02-03 per China MFA official list. STP domestic implementing instrument: Decree n. 09/2017 of 25 April 2017. The Chinese MFA official source specifies 30 days and covers 'passports for public affairs' in addition to diplomatic and service passports.

cbi.program_name: São Tomé and Príncipe Citizenship by Investment Program

options.notes: Non-refundable contribution to the National Transformation Fund. Single applicant: USD 90,000. Family of 2–4: USD 95,000. Each additional dependent: USD 5,000. Citizenship documents per dependent: USD 750. Submission fee: USD 5,000 per application. Post-approval addition fees: spouse USD 10,000; qualifying dependent USD 5,000; newborn (up to 1 year) USD 500. Program governed by Nationality Law No. 07/2022 and Decree-Law No. 07/2025. No real estate investment option currently available.

cbi.processing_time: 1.5–3 months to decision (official CIP site states 'approvals in as little as two months'); post-approval passport issuance approximately 3 months
cbi.notes: The program was officially launched on August 1, 2025, with practical acceptance of applications starting September 2025. 220+ applications received since launch as of late 2025. Administered by the Citizenship Investment Unit (CIU) under a public-private partnership, headquartered in Dubai, UAE. Applications must be submitted via a licensed Marketing Agent. Citizenship is granted for life and can be passed to future generations. Dual citizenship explicitly allowed. Physical visit to São Tomé and Príncipe is not required. Legal basis: Nationality Law No. 07/2022 and Decree-Law No. 07/2025. Official government portal: cip.gov.st (.gov.st domain). Blockchain-enabled citizenship system launched September 2025.

agreements: CPLP (Community of Portuguese Language Countries) - free movement and residency rights among member states
Bilateral visa exemption agreements with: Angola, Cabo Verde, Mozambique, Guinea-Bissau, Ghana, South Africa, Gabon, Rwanda

CPLP diplomatic/service passport waiver (Presidential Decree n. 21/2002): cited only by non-official aggregator (saotomeislands.com); official STP gazette text not directly verified.
Serbia bilateral agreement (signed 6 July 2022 for diplomatic/service passports): referenced only by Wikipedia and aggregators; not confirmed on any official STP government source.

Togo (TGO) visa exemption agreement: signed 18 June 2024 but NOT yet ratified as of 2026-06-29 (re-verified). Confirmed pending-ratification by Fragomen (implementation date not announced) and Wikipedia (updated June 2026). Do NOT add to visa_free until ratification confirmed on an official STP government source. Sources: https://www.fragomen.com/insights/sao-tome-and-principetogo-reciprocal-visa-waiver-agreement-signed.html; https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Visa_policy_of_S%C3%A3o_Tom%C3%A9_and_Pr%C3%ADncipe

No OCI (Overseas Citizen of India) or similar diaspora special-status provision found for STP.
The SMF portal URL https://www.smf.st/portal/visa-policy returned HTTP 404 - the visa policy notice at idnews=20 remains the primary official source.
No updated visa policy announcement from 2026 found on official SMF sources; the idnews=20 notice remains the most recent official document.
Official SMF e-visa portal does not publish specific fee amounts in USD - payment collected at border. Fees for business, work, student, and residence visa subcategories are not published online.

The SMF portal uses a single Autorizacao de Entrada form without distinguishing tourist/business/work subcategories - subcategory breakdown in visa_types is inferred from the legal framework and typical practice, not from explicitly labeled form fields.
Work permit and student visa specific requirements/fees not found on any official online source; applicants directed to contact SMF directly.
mne.gov.st availability in this session: not re-tested (returned HTTP 503 in prior session).
Saint Kitts and Nevis (KNA): Wikipedia states a visa waiver agreement was signed with STP but is pending ratification. Not in visa_free; flagged for future monitoring.
